title: Variables & Data Types
summary: Variables store information your code can use.
You create them with let or const.
const cannot be reassigned, let can.
Values have types, like text, numbers, and true or false.

sections.text: Programs need to remember things. A score, a name, whether a light is on. A variable is a named box where your code keeps a piece of information so it can use it later.

sections.title: What a Variable Is
sections.text: A variable is a named container for a value. You give it a name, put a value in, then use the name whenever you need that value. In modern JavaScript you make one with the word let or const.

sections.title: let vs const, and Types
sections.text: Use const when the value should not be reassigned, and let when it might change later. The value inside has a type. Common types are a string for text, a number for figures, and a boolean which is just true or false.

media.alt: A const box sealed and a let box being refilled

sections.title: Labeled Jars in a Kitchen
sections.text: Think of labeled jars on a shelf. The label is the variable name, like sugar. What is inside is the value. A const jar is sealed, you do not swap its contents. A let jar you can empty and refill. And jars hold different things, flour, sugar, salt, just like values have different types.

media.alt: Labeled kitchen jars, one sealed and one being refilled

sections.title: Making a Variable
items: Give the variable a clear name.
Use an equals sign to set its starting value.
Use the name elsewhere to read the value.
With let, you can give it a new value later.

sections.text: There is an older keyword, var, that you will still see in old code. Modern JavaScript prefers let and const because they behave more predictably.

terms.definition: A named container for a value.
A variable whose value should not be reassigned.
A variable whose value can change later.
A value that is true or false.

options: Stores information your code can use
questions.explanation: A variable is a named container that stores a value for your code to use.

questions.text: Which keyword stops a value from being reassigned?
questions.explanation: const is used when the value should not be reassigned.

options: A value that is true or false
questions.explanation: A boolean is a value that is either true or false.
